sizing_rule.note: Optimal f derived parametrically from generating function of historical trade data, bounded at 3 standard deviations on lower end. Position size = optimal_f × account_size / largest_loss. Example optimal_f = 0.206 when lower bound = 3 sigmas. Uses 100 equally spaced data points across bounded range.

source_rule_text: Position sizing should be determined by optimal f derived from a generating function bounded by parametric limits (lower bound at 3 sigmas, upper bound variable), using empirical trade data to calculate the optimal fraction for sizing subsequent trades.
